notes: R16 stays same-scope and merges the exact-admitted R8 and R15 surfaces instead of opening a wider precision sweep.
The bounded grid stays on memory streams only so the lane saturates the admitted real-trace precision surface without widening to arbitrary streams or stack-wide guarantees.
Default base 64 defines the screened grid before any boundary-bearing stream receives a small decomposition base sweep.
Base 256 remains a weaker negative control rather than a positive alternative.

effective_here: The admitted same-scope R8/R15 memory surface remains precision-stable on 8/8 screened streams under the bounded grid.
unproven_here: 0/8 screened streams still need decomposition recovery or remain boundary-bearing, so broader robustness remains unproven.
negated_here: Single-head float32 fails without screened recovery on 0/8 screened streams, and the weaker base-256 control fails on 0/1 boundary-bearing follow-ups.

supported_here: R16 saturates the admitted same-scope real-trace precision surface by merging the exact-admitted R8 and R15 families without widening endpoint scope.
The lane keeps failure taxonomy, decomposition recovery, and weaker negative controls explicit at stream and family granularity before runtime follow-up work resumes.
unsupported_here: R16 does not justify unseen-family precision guarantees, a broader stack-wide robustness claim, or arbitrary compiled-language support.
R16 does not by itself establish same-endpoint runtime superiority; that remains downstream of R17.
